name: Zombie
description: A body that doesn't know it's dead. Slow, relentless, and deeply upsetting because it used to be someone.

sensory.sight: a person walking wrong — limbs at bad angles, head tilted, but still moving, still coming
sensory.sound: a groan that isn't pain or speech, just air being pushed through a throat that doesn't breathe
sensory.feel: dread, not because it's fast (it isn't) but because it doesn't stop

encounter_hooks: a figure stumbling along the road at night, not responding to calls
a villager who died last week, standing in their own field
scratching at a barred door, patient and endless
notes: Zombies are tragic. Somebody loved this person. In Threadhallow, zombies might be Fray-animated corpses (the weave trying to repair death) or weave-memories with just enough pattern to move a body.
